key_insight: IPSM-Bench introduces a large, high-quality benchmark for segmenting intermediate phases in zinc-alloy microstructures, and SCoP-SAM boosts segmentation by injecting spatial context priors into SAM.
engineering_relevance: Better intermediate-phase segmentation can improve microstructure analysis, helping guide the design and performance optimization of zinc-based absorbable biomaterials.
method: Built IPSM-Bench, a new annotated dataset for zinc-alloy intermediate phase segmentation
Designed SCoP-SAM to use gradient and grayscale cues as spatial context priors
Integrated these priors into the SAM encoding-decoding pipeline for improved segmentation
evidence_signal: The authors report extensive experiments on IPSM-Bench and additional public alloy benchmarks, where SCoP-SAM achieves state-of-the-art performance and strong cross-benchmark generalization.
watch_item: Performance gains may depend on image quality and the availability of reliable grayscale/gradient cues, so robustness across varied microscopy conditions should be validated.
paper_title: IPSM-Bench: A New Intermediate Phase Segmentation Benchmark in Microstructure Images of Zinc-Based Absorbable Biomaterials
